content: Edge case: a single user turn can contain a complete design document ('spec drop'). This inverts the assumed pattern of agent-proposes/user-confirms. The tree needs to handle cases where the user turn is the substantive one and the agent turn is just acknowledgement. A single pair may also contain multiple distinct signals — the tree needs to handle multi-signal pairs (e.g. seq 10 had ~8 decisions in one user turn).

provenance.raw_agent: seq 10 — a single user turn containing a complete design document. That's not really a 'pair' in the ratification sense, it's a spec drop. The tree needs to handle the case where the user turn is the substantive one and the agent turn is just acknowledgement.
provenance.raw_user: I think it's fine. We can also look at .pi/inbox to try to judge what would make sense.
